***

Hi Sam. It's okay. I'm here with you. Sometimes our feelings get really, really big. They feel like a giant storm inside. That's okay. It happens to lots of kids. I'm going to help you through this storm. We're going to do it together.

Good job. Your body is listening to you.

Now, I want you to look around the room you're in. Don't move from your spot. Just look with your eyes. I want you to find five things that are the color blue. Or green. Or any color you like. Can you see one thing? Name it quietly in your head. "A blue book." Good. Find another. "A green pillow." Keep looking. Find five things. Take your time...

This is helping your busy brain focus on something else for a minute. You're doing great.

Sometimes, big feelings need to get out in a safe way. I have a safe way for us to try. It's called the Volcano. First, make your hands into tight fists. Squeeze them as hard as you can. Squeeze all the mad or scared or frustrated energy into your fists. Hold it... hold it... Now, take a big breath in... and as you breathe out, explode your hands open! Push all that energy out through your fingertips. Whoosh! Let it all go.

Let's do it two more times. Make the fists... squeeze tight... breathe in... and explode out! Whoosh! One more time. Fists... squeeze... breathe in... and... explode! Feel that energy leaving your body.

Good. Now, let's shake it all out. Shake your hands like they're wet and you're trying to flick off all the water. Shake shake shake. Shake your arms. Shake your whole body for a few seconds. And stop. Let everything be still.

Take a normal breath now. Just in... and out.

I want you to imagine something for me. Imagine your favorite stuffed animal or a super soft blanket. Can you picture it? Think about how it feels. Is it fuzzy? Is it smooth? Is it cool or warm? Imagine holding it close. Imagine giving it a little squeeze. Feel that soft, safe feeling in your hands. You can even hug your own arms right now if you want to. Give yourself a soft, safe squeeze.

You are doing such a good job. You are being so brave.

Let's check in with your body one more time. How do your feet feel on the floor now? Are your shoulders a little looser? Take one more big breath in... and let it all the way out with a sigh. A big "haaaaaaa" sound. Let that last little bit of the storm cloud float away.

Remember, Sam, big feelings are like weather. They come, and they always, always go. You rode through this storm. You used your strong feet. You used your powerful breath. You shook the energy out. You are safe.

When you feel calm like this, it's a good time to find a grown-up you trust. You can tell them, "I had a big feeling, and I used my tools." They will be so proud of you. I am proud of you.

Keep being kind to yourself. You've got this. Goodbye for now.
